task_name: FLS Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery Overview - Educational Content
reason_no_score: This is an instructional/educational video presenting an overview of the FLS program, its history, structure, and importance. It contains no actual task performance—no trainer box footage, no live instrument work, no timed task execution. Video consists entirely of animated slides, infographics, text overlays, and conceptual diagrams.

video_structure.content: Quote: 'See one, do one, teach one' — teaching philosophy
Part 1: Cognitive Exam (multiple-choice), Part 2: Manual Skills Exam (hands-on simulator test)
Diagram showing Task 1 (Peg Transfer), Task 2 (Precision Cutting), Task 3 (Ligating Loop), Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture), Task 5 (Intracorporeal Suture) with associated surgical competencies
Lists: angled laparoscope use, bimanual dexterity, traction/countertraction, tissue ligation, knot tying
Explanation that training is based on achieving expert performance level, not time-based
Statistic: Trainees achieved proficiency after average of 9.7 hours of practice; 100% success rate shown
Illustration of FLS Trainer Box as flight simulator for surgeons
Mandatory for ABS and ABOG board certification; ensures validated baseline skill; optimizes patient safety
Proficiency-based training results in durable skills retained for at least one year
Three-step pathway: Step 1 (Complete Residency), Step 2 (Achieve FLS Certification), Step 3 (Become Board Certified)
Final slide: 'FLS standardized today's essential skills... what will tomorrow's be?'

demonstrated_techniques: Overview of five FLS tasks and their technical objectives
Mapping of individual tasks to seven core surgical competencies
Introduction to the FLS Trainer Box simulator equipment
verbal_cues: 'See one, do one, teach one' — foundational teaching philosophy
Proficiency-based training emphasizes achieving expert performance level rather than time spent
Data-driven: average 9.7 hours of practice to achieve proficiency
Skills retained for at least one year with proficiency-based approach
teaching_content.educational_focus: Program introduction and justification for FLS as mandatory training

confidence_rationale: Unambiguous classification as educational content. No performance footage, no task execution, no timing possible. Purely instructional with narration and animated slides.
cannot_determine: Task identification — no actual task is being performed
Performance metrics — no trainer box or live instruments visible
Completion time — no task execution present
